features.note: Replicable two ways: (a) app computes pace from CoreLocation and fires WKInterfaceDevice.play(.notification)/haptics on threshold; (b) WorkoutKit PaceRangeAlert / SpeedRangeAlert attached to a custom workout step, with the system delivering the alert (watchOS 11 even added indoor-run pace alerts). Fully supported for third parties.

features.name: Heart-rate alerts (zone/range)
features.what: Alert when HR exits a target zone or crosses high/low thresholds.
features.note: Replicable. Live HR from HKLiveWorkoutBuilder (HKQuantityTypeIdentifier.heartRate); app fires haptics on threshold, or use WorkoutKit HeartRateRangeAlert / HeartRateZoneAlert in a structured workout. No Firstbeat needed for a simple zone alert.

features.name: Power alerts (target/range)
features.what: Alert when running/cycling power leaves a target range or threshold.
features.note: Replicable. WorkoutKit PowerRangeAlert / PowerThresholdAlert supports current and (watchOS 10.4+) average power alerts for running and cycling. Power source is Apple native runningPower or a paired BLE cycling power meter. Third-party supported.

features.name: Cadence alerts (target/range)
features.what: Alert when running/cycling cadence leaves a target range.
features.note: Replicable via WorkoutKit CadenceRangeAlert / CadenceThresholdAlert, or app-side from CMPedometer/step data + haptics.

features.name: Virtual Partner / Virtual Pacer
features.what: An on-screen avatar/competitor running at a set pace; shows live distance ahead/behind versus that target pace.
features.note: Replicable as pure app logic + UI: compare elapsed-time-vs-distance against a constant target pace and render an ahead/behind gap. Apple's own 'Pacer' workout does exactly this. No Garmin sensor or Firstbeat involved — it's arithmetic over pace and time. Garmin's branded avatar UI is the only non-portable part.

features.name: Structured workouts / interval workouts (warmup, work, recovery, repeats, cooldown with per-step targets)
features.what: Pre-built workout executed live: stepped intervals with per-step duration/distance targets and pace/HR/power targets, auto-advance between steps, step alerts.
features.note: Largely replicable via WorkoutKit (iOS17/watchOS10+): CustomWorkout with warmup/work/recovery/cooldown steps, IntervalBlock repeats, and per-step PaceRange/HeartRate/Power/Cadence alerts; can be scheduled into Apple's Workout app. Two real gaps: (1) richest control runs through the SYSTEM Workout app, and a third-party app driving its OWN HKWorkoutSession must implement step sequencing/auto-advance manually; (2) developers report difficulty reading back the executed interval structure/splits from a system-recorded structured run. So feature exists but with less depth/flexibility than Garmin's workout engine.

features.name: Multisport (auto/manual sport transitions, e.g. triathlon swim-bike-run in one activity)
features.what: Single recorded activity spanning multiple sports with transition timing; one combined file.
features.note: Apple supports Multisport natively (swim-bike-run with auto transitions) and WorkoutKit/HealthKit can represent multisport, but third-party control is limited: a custom app can chain multiple HKWorkoutSessions/activity types and stitch them, yet seamless AUTO transition detection between sports is a system/Workout-app feature not cleanly exposed for arbitrary third-party sport orders. Manual-transition multisport is achievable; Garmin-grade auto-transition + full per-leg data fields is harder.

features.name: Music controls during activity (play/pause/skip/volume)
features.what: On-watch transport controls for music playing on the watch or phone while recording.
features.note: Limited for third parties. watchOS does not give third-party apps a general MusicKit transport API like iOS; programmatic control/MPMusicPlayerController is constrained on watchOS, and remote-command control of other apps' playback is restricted. The system 'Now Playing' surface handles transport, but a custom workout app embedding full play/pause/skip/volume for arbitrary players is not reliably available. Apple intends Now Playing (separate app/dock), not in-app, to own this.
